metadata.name: Enterprise Risk Management Ontology
metadata.description: Domain-agnostic enterprise risk management ontology aligned to ISO 31000:2018. GRC Tier 1 domain governed by GRC-FW governance hub. 6 owned entities: RiskRegister, Risk, RiskAppetite, Control, ResilienceCapability, RiskAssessment. 23 risk categories including 9 AI-specific. 19 relationships (11 internal + 8 cross-ontology). 1 VSEM bridge. Satisfies grc-fw:governsRisk and grc-fw:assuranceAssessesRisk forward references.
metadata.purpose: Provide domain-agnostic enterprise risk management concepts enabling risk registers, risk appetite statements, control frameworks, resilience capabilities, and risk assessments. Tier 1 parent for domain-specific risk specialisations (ISO 27005, AI risk, operational risk).
metadata.creator: Azlan EA-AAA

oaa:sourceFrameworks.name: COBIT 2019 — APO12 (Managed Risk) & EDM03 (Ensure Risk Optimisation)
NCSC Cyber Governance Code — Principle A (Risk Management)
DORA — Digital Operational Resilience Act, Chapter II

oaa:imports.name: GRC-FW-ONT
oaa:imports.description: GRC governance hub — provides GRCContext and GovernanceAssurance for governance-to-risk bridges

oaa:imports.name: RMF-IS27005-ONT
oaa:imports.description: ISO 27005 information security risk management — Tier 2 specialisation below ERM

oaa:imports.name: NCSC-CAF-ONT
oaa:imports.description: NCSC Cyber Assessment Framework — CAF Objective A risk management alignment

oaa:imports.name: MCSB-ONT
oaa:imports.description: Microsoft Cloud Security Benchmark — security control alignment

oaa:imports.name: VSOM-ONT
oaa:imports.description: Vision & Strategic Objectives Model — VSEM bridge for risk-threatens-objective

oaa:joinPatterns.name: GRC Governance to Risk Register Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Governance context oversees enterprise risk registers — satisfies grc-fw:governsRisk forward reference
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Board-level visibility from governance context into enterprise risk register and individual risks

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Risk Assessment to Treatment Lifecycle
oaa:joinPatterns.description: ISO 31000 risk process: assessment identifies risks which are treated by controls
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: erm:RiskAssessment → erm:assessesRegister → erm:RiskRegister → erm:containsRisk → erm:Risk → erm:mitigatedByControl → erm:Control
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Full risk lifecycle from assessment through to control implementation

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Risk Appetite Governance Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Risk appetite constrains risk acceptance decisions within the governance context
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Ensure risk acceptance decisions are within approved appetite levels

oaa:joinPatterns.name: ERM to RMF Tier 1-to-Tier 2 Specialisation
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Domain-agnostic ERM risks specialise into ISO 27005 information security risks
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Navigate from enterprise risk register to domain-specific information security risk details

oaa:joinPatterns.name: ERM Control to MCSB Benchmark Alignment
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Enterprise controls align to Microsoft Cloud Security Benchmark controls
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Map enterprise-level controls to Azure/cloud security benchmark implementation guidance

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Risk Threatens Strategic Objective (VSEM Bridge)
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Enterprise risks threaten strategic objectives — enables risk-informed strategic planning
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Board-level risk-to-strategy alignment: which strategic objectives are at risk?

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Resilience Capability to Risk Coverage
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Resilience capabilities protect against specific risks and inform business continuity
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Determine resilience coverage for identified risks — RTO/RPO verification

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Assurance to Risk Assessment Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Governance assurance activities evaluate risks — satisfies grc-fw:assuranceAssessesRisk
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Link governance assurance activities to the risks they evaluate

entities.name: RiskRegister
entities.description: An enterprise risk register providing a structured repository for identified risks within a defined scope. Supports 7 scope levels from Enterprise to Vendor. Contains risks, references risk appetite, and tracks review cadence. ISO 31000:2018 clause 6.7 — recording and reporting. Satisfies grc-fw:governsRisk forward reference from GRC-FW-ONT v3.0.0.

properties.name: registerId
properties.description: Unique identifier for the risk register

properties.name: registerName
properties.description: Human-readable name for the risk register

properties.name: scope
properties.description: Scope level: Enterprise, Division, Business-Unit, Programme, Project, Third-Party, Vendor

properties.name: description
properties.description: Description of the register scope, purpose, and coverage boundaries

properties.name: owner
properties.description: Risk register owner — typically CRO, Head of Risk, or equivalent governance role

properties.name: riskAppetiteRef
properties.description: Reference to the RiskAppetite instance governing this register

properties.name: riskCount
properties.description: Total number of risks in the register

properties.name: criticalRiskCount
properties.description: Number of risks with residual risk level of Critical

properties.name: highRiskCount
properties.description: Number of risks with residual risk level of High

properties.name: lastReviewDate
properties.description: Date of the last formal review of the risk register

properties.name: nextReviewDate
properties.description: Scheduled date for the next review

properties.name: reviewFrequency
properties.description: Review cadence: Weekly, Monthly, Quarterly, Semi-Annual, Annual

properties.name: status
properties.description: Register status: Draft, Active, Under-Review, Archived

entities.name: Risk
entities.description: An identified enterprise risk expressed as the effect of uncertainty on objectives (ISO 31000:2018 definition). Uses a 5x5 likelihood-impact matrix for inherent and residual scoring. 23 risk categories covering traditional, technology, AI-specific, and operational risk domains. Supports 5 response strategies. Satisfies grc-fw:assuranceAssessesRisk forward reference from GRC-FW-ONT v3.0.0.

properties.name: riskId
properties.description: Unique identifier for the risk

properties.name: riskTitle
properties.description: Short descriptive title of the risk

properties.name: riskDescription
properties.description: Detailed description of the risk scenario, cause, and potential consequence

properties.name: riskCategory
properties.description: Risk category from 23-value taxonomy (see RiskCategory enumeration)

properties.name: inherentLikelihood
properties.description: Inherent likelihood score before controls (1=Rare, 2=Unlikely, 3=Possible, 4=Likely, 5=Almost-Certain)

properties.name: inherentImpact
properties.description: Inherent impact score before controls (1=Negligible, 2=Minor, 3=Moderate, 4=Major, 5=Catastrophic)

properties.name: inherentRiskScore
properties.description: Calculated inherent risk score (likelihood x impact, range 1-25)

properties.name: inherentRiskLevel
properties.description: Derived inherent risk level: Low (1-4), Medium (5-9), High (10-15), Critical (16-25)

properties.name: residualLikelihood
properties.description: Residual likelihood score after controls applied (1-5)

properties.name: residualImpact
properties.description: Residual impact score after controls applied (1-5)

properties.name: residualRiskScore
properties.description: Calculated residual risk score (likelihood x impact, range 1-25)

properties.name: residualRiskLevel
properties.description: Derived residual risk level: Low, Medium, High, Critical

properties.name: velocity
properties.description: Speed at which the risk could materialise: Immediate, Days, Weeks, Months, Years

properties.name: trend
properties.description: Directional trend of the risk: Increasing, Stable, Decreasing, New

properties.name: response
properties.description: Risk response strategy: Avoid, Mitigate, Transfer, Accept, Exploit

properties.name: riskOwner
properties.description: Person or role accountable for managing this risk

properties.name: threatensObjectiveRef
properties.description: Reference to the strategic objective(s) this risk threatens

properties.name: escalationTrigger
properties.description: Condition that triggers escalation to a higher governance body

properties.name: riskStatus
properties.description: Lifecycle status: Identified, Assessed, Treating, Accepted, Closed, Escalated

properties.name: dateIdentified
properties.description: Date the risk was first identified

properties.name: lastAssessedDate
properties.description: Date of the last formal risk assessment for this risk

entities.name: RiskAppetite
entities.description: An organisational risk appetite statement defining the amount and type of risk the organisation is willing to accept in pursuit of its objectives (ISO 31000:2018 clause 3.7). Aligned to NCSC Cyber Governance Code Principle A — board sets risk appetite.

properties.name: appetiteId
properties.description: Unique identifier for this risk appetite statement

properties.name: appetiteName
properties.description: Name of the appetite statement

properties.name: appetiteLevel
properties.description: Overall organisational risk appetite: Averse, Minimalist, Cautious, Open, Hungry

properties.name: toleranceThreshold
properties.description: Maximum tolerable risk level before mandatory escalation: Low, Medium, High, Critical

properties.name: categoryAppetites
properties.description: Per-category appetite overrides as structured text

properties.name: approvedBy
properties.description: Governance body or individual that approved this appetite statement

properties.name: approvalDate
properties.description: Date the appetite statement was approved

properties.name: reviewDate
properties.description: Next scheduled review date

properties.name: scope
properties.description: Scope this appetite applies to: Enterprise, Division, Business-Unit, Programme

properties.name: status
properties.description: Statement status: Draft, Approved, Under-Review, Superseded

entities.name: Control
entities.description: An enterprise-level control or safeguard implemented to modify risk (ISO 31000:2018 clause 3.8). Domain-agnostic Tier 1 parent — RMF-IS27005 Control (ISO 27001 Annex A) specialises this. Single authoritative source for controls across GRC; SEC-FW and COMP-FW reference rather than duplicate.

properties.name: controlId
properties.description: Unique identifier for the control

properties.name: controlName
properties.description: Name of the control

properties.name: controlDescription
properties.description: Detailed description of the control purpose, scope, and operation

properties.name: controlType
properties.description: Control type: Preventive, Detective, Corrective, Deterrent, Compensating

properties.name: controlNature
properties.description: Control nature: Technical, Administrative, Physical, Legal

properties.name: automationLevel
properties.description: Automation level: Manual, Semi-Automated, Automated, Continuous, AI-Driven

properties.name: effectiveness
properties.description: Assessed effectiveness score (1=Ineffective, 2=Partially-Effective, 3=Effective, 4=Highly-Effective, 5=Optimised)

properties.name: testingFrequency
properties.description: How often the control is tested: Continuous, Monthly, Quarterly, Semi-Annual, Annual, Ad-Hoc

properties.name: lastTestDate
properties.description: Date of the last control effectiveness test

properties.name: nextTestDate
properties.description: Next scheduled test date

properties.name: implementationStatus
properties.description: Implementation status: Planned, In-Progress, Implemented, Operational, Decommissioned

properties.name: controlOwner
properties.description: Person or role responsible for the control

properties.name: riskReduction
properties.description: Expected risk reduction: Likelihood-Only, Impact-Only, Both, Neither

entities.name: ResilienceCapability
entities.description: An organisational resilience capability defining recovery and continuity posture. Aligned to FCA/PRA Operational Resilience (Important Business Services, impact tolerances), DORA Chapter II (ICT risk management), and NIS2 Article 21. 8 resilience types including 2 AI-specific.

properties.name: capabilityId
properties.description: Unique identifier for the resilience capability

properties.name: capabilityName
properties.description: Name of the resilience capability

properties.name: resilienceType
properties.description: Resilience type: Business-Continuity, Disaster-Recovery, Cyber-Resilience, Operational-Resilience, Supply-Chain-Resilience, Data-Resilience, AI-Model-Failover, AI-Pipeline-Recovery

properties.name: rto
properties.description: Recovery Time Objective — maximum acceptable downtime

properties.name: rpo
properties.description: Recovery Point Objective — maximum acceptable data loss

properties.name: impactTolerance
properties.description: FCA/PRA impact tolerance — maximum tolerable disruption

properties.name: importantBusinessService
properties.description: FCA/PRA Important Business Service this capability protects

properties.name: testResult
properties.description: Most recent test result: Passed, Passed-With-Issues, Failed, Not-Tested

properties.name: lastTestDate
properties.description: Date of the last resilience test

properties.name: nextTestDate
properties.description: Scheduled date for the next test

properties.name: degradedModeCapability
properties.description: Whether the service can operate in a degraded mode while recovering

properties.name: degradedModeDescription
properties.description: Description of degraded mode capabilities and limitations

properties.name: owner
properties.description: Person or role responsible for maintaining this resilience capability

properties.name: status
properties.description: Capability status: Planned, Implementing, Operational, Tested, Needs-Improvement

entities.name: RiskAssessment
entities.description: A structured enterprise risk assessment exercise encompassing risk identification, analysis, and evaluation (ISO 31000:2018 clause 6.4). Domain-agnostic parent of RMF-IS27005 RiskAssessment. Bridge target for NCSC-CAF JP-CAF-003 alignment.

properties.name: assessmentId
properties.description: Unique identifier for the risk assessment

properties.name: assessmentName
properties.description: Name or title of the assessment exercise

properties.name: assessmentType
properties.description: Assessment type: Enterprise-Wide, Domain-Specific, Project, Vendor, AI-System, Regulatory-Change, Incident-Triggered

properties.name: methodology
properties.description: Methodology: Qualitative, Quantitative, Semi-Quantitative, Scenario-Based, Bow-Tie

properties.name: assessmentDate
properties.description: Date the assessment was conducted

properties.name: assessor
properties.description: Person, team, or external party conducting the assessment

properties.name: assessmentStatus
properties.description: Status: Planned, In-Progress, Completed, Approved, Superseded

properties.name: risksIdentified
properties.description: Number of risks identified during the assessment

properties.name: newRisksCount
properties.description: Number of newly identified risks

properties.name: closedRisksCount
properties.description: Number of risks closed as a result of the assessment

properties.name: approvedBy
properties.description: Governance body or individual who approved the assessment results

properties.name: nextReviewDate
properties.description: Scheduled date for the next periodic assessment

properties.name: scope
properties.description: Scope: Enterprise, Division, Business-Unit, Programme, Project, Third-Party, Vendor

entities.name: GRCContext
entities.description: External GRC-FW entity — top-level governance context that oversees risk registers

entities.name: GovernanceAssurance
entities.description: External GRC-FW entity — assurance activity that evaluates risks

entities.name: Risk (RMF-IS27005)
entities.description: External RMF-IS27005 entity — ISO 27005 information security risk specialisation of erm:Risk

entities.name: Control (RMF-IS27005)
entities.description: External RMF-IS27005 entity — ISO 27001 Annex A security control specialisation of erm:Control

entities.name: RiskAssessment (RMF-IS27005)
entities.description: External RMF-IS27005 entity — ISO 27005 risk assessment specialisation of erm:RiskAssessment

entities.name: ContributingOutcome
entities.description: External NCSC-CAF entity — CAF Objective A contributing outcomes for risk management alignment

entities.name: SecurityControl
entities.description: External MCSB entity — Microsoft Cloud Security Benchmark control

entities.name: StrategicObjective
entities.description: External VSOM entity — VSEM bridge target for risk-threatens-objective alignment

relationships.name: Contains Risk
relationships.description: Links a RiskRegister to the individual risks it contains

relationships.name: Belongs To Register
relationships.description: Links a Risk to the RiskRegister it belongs to

relationships.name: Has Appetite
relationships.description: Links a RiskRegister to its governing risk appetite statement

relationships.name: Mitigated By Control
relationships.description: Links a Risk to the controls that mitigate it

relationships.name: Control Mitigates Risk
relationships.description: Inverse — links a Control to the risks it mitigates

relationships.name: Protects Against Risk
relationships.description: Links a ResilienceCapability to the risks it provides protection for

relationships.name: Has Resilience Capability
relationships.description: Links a Risk to the resilience capabilities that provide recovery should it materialise

relationships.name: Assesses Register
relationships.description: Links a RiskAssessment to the RiskRegister it evaluates

relationships.name: Assessment Identifies Risk
relationships.description: Links a RiskAssessment to individual risks identified during the assessment

relationships.name: Control Tested By Assessment
relationships.description: Links a Control to a RiskAssessment that tests its effectiveness

relationships.name: Resilience Tested By Assessment
relationships.description: Links a ResilienceCapability to a RiskAssessment that validates it

relationships.name: Governed By
relationships.description: Links a RiskRegister to the GRCContext that governs it — reverse of grc-fw:governsRisk

relationships.name: Assessed By Assurance
relationships.description: Links a Risk to governance assurance activities that evaluate it — reverse of grc-fw:assuranceAssessesRisk

relationships.name: Specialised By InfoSec Risk
relationships.description: Links an enterprise Risk to its ISO 27005 information security risk specialisation — Tier 1 to Tier 2

relationships.name: Specialised By InfoSec Assessment
relationships.description: Links an enterprise RiskAssessment to its ISO 27005 assessment specialisation — Tier 1 to Tier 2

relationships.name: Specialised By InfoSec Control
relationships.description: Links an enterprise Control to its ISO 27001 Annex A security control specialisation — Tier 1 to Tier 2

relationships.name: Assessed By CAF Outcome
relationships.description: Links a RiskRegister to NCSC CAF Objective A contributing outcomes for cyber risk management alignment

relationships.name: Control Aligns To Benchmark
relationships.description: Links an enterprise Control to MCSB SecurityControl for cloud security benchmark alignment

relationships.name: Risk Threatens Objective
relationships.description: VSEM bridge — links a Risk to the strategic objectives it threatens, enabling risk-informed strategic planning

businessRules.name: Risk Requires Register
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have exactly one belongsToRegister relationship to a RiskRegister

businessRules.name: Inherent Risk Score Calculation
businessRules.condition: IF a Risk has inherentLikelihood and inherentImpact
businessRules.action: THEN inherentRiskScore MUST equal inherentLikelihood * inherentImpact AND inherentRiskLevel MUST be derived (1-4=Low, 5-9=Medium, 10-15=High, 16-25=Critical)

businessRules.name: Residual Risk Score Calculation
businessRules.condition: IF a Risk has residualLikelihood and residualImpact
businessRules.action: THEN residualRiskScore MUST equal residualLikelihood * residualImpact AND residualRiskLevel MUST be derived consistently

businessRules.name: Critical Risk Requires Response
businessRules.condition: IF a Risk has inherentRiskLevel of Critical
businessRules.action: THEN response MUST be specified and MUST NOT be Accept unless approved at board level

businessRules.name: Mitigate Response Requires Controls
businessRules.condition: IF a Risk has response of Mitigate
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have at least one mitigatedByControl relationship to a Control with implementationStatus of Implemented or Operational

businessRules.name: Accept Response Requires Appetite Validation
businessRules.condition: IF a Risk has response of Accept
businessRules.action: THEN residualRiskLevel MUST be at or below the RiskAppetite.toleranceThreshold of the parent register

businessRules.name: Register Requires Governance Link
businessRules.condition: IF a RiskRegister is created with scope of Enterprise or Division
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have a governedBy relationship to a grc-fw:GRCContext

businessRules.name: Risk Appetite Requires Approval
businessRules.condition: IF a RiskAppetite has status of Approved
businessRules.action: THEN approvedBy and approvalDate MUST be specified

businessRules.name: Resilience Capability Requires RTO RPO
businessRules.action: THEN rto and rpo MUST be specified

businessRules.name: Control Effectiveness Requires Testing
businessRules.condition: IF a Control has effectiveness score specified
businessRules.action: THEN lastTestDate MUST be specified — effectiveness claims require evidence

businessRules.name: Assessment Must Link To Register
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have exactly one assessesRegister relationship to a RiskRegister

businessRules.name: Risk Category Validation
businessRules.condition: IF a Risk has riskCategory specified
businessRules.action: THEN riskCategory MUST be one of the 23 defined values in the RiskCategory enumeration

businessRules.name: Escalation Trigger for Critical Risks
businessRules.condition: IF a Risk has residualRiskLevel of Critical or trend of Increasing with residualRiskLevel of High
businessRules.action: THEN escalationTrigger MUST be specified AND riskStatus SHOULD be Escalated

businessRules.name: Operational Resilience Impact Tolerance
businessRules.condition: IF a ResilienceCapability has resilienceType of Operational-Resilience
businessRules.action: THEN impactTolerance and importantBusinessService SHOULD be specified (FCA/PRA PS21/3)

oaa:enumerations.name: RegisterScope
oaa:enumerations.description: Scope levels for risk registers — 7 levels from enterprise to vendor

hasDefinedTerm.name: Enterprise
hasDefinedTerm.description: Enterprise-wide risk register

hasDefinedTerm.name: Division
hasDefinedTerm.description: Division-level risk register

hasDefinedTerm.name: Business-Unit
hasDefinedTerm.description: Business unit or department level

hasDefinedTerm.name: Programme
hasDefinedTerm.description: Programme-level risk register

hasDefinedTerm.name: Project
hasDefinedTerm.description: Project-level risk register

hasDefinedTerm.name: Third-Party
hasDefinedTerm.description: Third-party or outsourced service

hasDefinedTerm.name: Vendor
hasDefinedTerm.description: Vendor-specific risk register

oaa:enumerations.name: RiskCategory
oaa:enumerations.description: 23-value risk category taxonomy. Grouping: Traditional (5), Technology (3), AI-Specific (9), Operational-Extended (6).

hasDefinedTerm.name: Strategic
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risks to strategic direction, market position, and competitive advantage

hasDefinedTerm.name: Operational
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risks to business processes, people, systems, and operations

hasDefinedTerm.name: Financial
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risks to revenue, cash flow, liquidity, credit, and financial performance

hasDefinedTerm.name: Compliance
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risks from regulatory non-compliance, legal obligations, and contractual breaches

hasDefinedTerm.name: Reputational
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risks to brand, stakeholder confidence, and public trust

hasDefinedTerm.name: Technology
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risks from technology failure, obsolescence, and infrastructure dependency

hasDefinedTerm.name: Cybersecurity
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risks from cyber threats, data breaches, and security incidents

hasDefinedTerm.name: Data-Quality
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risks from poor data quality, integrity, and governance

hasDefinedTerm.name: AI-Model-Performance
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risks from AI model degradation, drift, accuracy loss

hasDefinedTerm.name: AI-Bias-Fairness
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risks from AI bias, discrimination, and unfair outcomes — EU AI Act Art 10, 15

hasDefinedTerm.name: AI-Safety
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risks from AI systems causing harm to individuals or the environment

hasDefinedTerm.name: AI-Explainability
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risks from inability to explain AI decisions

hasDefinedTerm.name: AI-Data-Poisoning
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risks from malicious manipulation of training data

hasDefinedTerm.name: AI-Adversarial
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risks from adversarial attacks on AI systems

hasDefinedTerm.name: AI-Hallucination
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risks from AI-generated content that is factually incorrect or fabricated

hasDefinedTerm.name: Supply-Chain-AI
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risks from AI dependency in supply chain — vendor model lock-in, data provenance

hasDefinedTerm.name: Shadow-AI
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risks from unapproved or ungoverned AI usage

hasDefinedTerm.name: Vendor-Dependency
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risks from excessive reliance on single vendors

hasDefinedTerm.name: Skills-Gap
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risks from workforce capability gaps and talent retention

hasDefinedTerm.name: Change-Management
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risks from inadequate change management and transformation failure

hasDefinedTerm.name: Business-Continuity
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risks to business continuity from disruption or disaster

hasDefinedTerm.name: Regulatory-Change
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risks from regulatory changes and compliance deadline pressures

hasDefinedTerm.name: Third-Party
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risks from third-party relationships and outsourcing

hasDefinedTerm.name: Avoid
hasDefinedTerm.description: Eliminate the risk by removing the activity or cause

hasDefinedTerm.name: Mitigate
hasDefinedTerm.description: Reduce likelihood or impact through controls

hasDefinedTerm.name: Transfer
hasDefinedTerm.description: Transfer risk to a third party (insurance, outsourcing, contractual)

hasDefinedTerm.name: Accept
hasDefinedTerm.description: Accept the risk within appetite — requires appropriate authority

hasDefinedTerm.name: Exploit
hasDefinedTerm.description: Actively pursue the risk for strategic opportunity (upside risk)

oaa:enumerations.name: AppetiteLevel
oaa:enumerations.description: Risk appetite levels from most conservative to most aggressive

hasDefinedTerm.name: Averse
hasDefinedTerm.description: Avoidance of risk — accept only negligible residual risk

hasDefinedTerm.name: Minimalist
hasDefinedTerm.description: Accept minimal risk — preference for ultra-safe options

hasDefinedTerm.name: Cautious
hasDefinedTerm.description: Accept moderate risk — preference for safe delivery

hasDefinedTerm.name: Open
hasDefinedTerm.description: Accept higher risk — willing to consider all options

hasDefinedTerm.name: Hungry
hasDefinedTerm.description: Actively seek risk — eager to pursue opportunities

hasDefinedTerm.name: Preventive
hasDefinedTerm.description: Prevents the risk event from occurring

hasDefinedTerm.name: Detective
hasDefinedTerm.description: Detects the risk event when it occurs

hasDefinedTerm.name: Corrective
hasDefinedTerm.description: Corrects the impact after the risk event

hasDefinedTerm.name: Deterrent
hasDefinedTerm.description: Deters threat actors from exploiting a vulnerability

hasDefinedTerm.name: Compensating
hasDefinedTerm.description: Alternative control when the primary is not feasible

oaa:enumerations.name: ControlNature
oaa:enumerations.description: Control nature classification by implementation mechanism

hasDefinedTerm.name: Technical
hasDefinedTerm.description: Technology-based control

hasDefinedTerm.name: Administrative
hasDefinedTerm.description: Policy, procedure, or process-based control

hasDefinedTerm.name: Physical
hasDefinedTerm.description: Physical security control

hasDefinedTerm.name: Legal
hasDefinedTerm.description: Legal or contractual control

oaa:enumerations.name: AutomationLevel
oaa:enumerations.description: Control automation spectrum from manual to AI-driven

hasDefinedTerm.name: Manual
hasDefinedTerm.description: Entirely human-operated

hasDefinedTerm.name: Semi-Automated
hasDefinedTerm.description: Partially automated with human oversight

hasDefinedTerm.name: Automated
hasDefinedTerm.description: Fully automated execution

hasDefinedTerm.name: Continuous
hasDefinedTerm.description: Continuous automated monitoring and response

hasDefinedTerm.name: AI-Driven
hasDefinedTerm.description: AI/ML-driven adaptive control

hasDefinedTerm.name: Business-Continuity
hasDefinedTerm.description: Business continuity planning — ISO 22301

hasDefinedTerm.name: Disaster-Recovery
hasDefinedTerm.description: IT disaster recovery with RTO/RPO targets

hasDefinedTerm.name: Cyber-Resilience
hasDefinedTerm.description: Cyber incident response and recovery — NIST CSF Recover

hasDefinedTerm.name: Operational-Resilience
hasDefinedTerm.description: FCA/PRA operational resilience — IBS and impact tolerances

hasDefinedTerm.name: Supply-Chain-Resilience
hasDefinedTerm.description: Supply chain and third-party continuity

hasDefinedTerm.name: Data-Resilience
hasDefinedTerm.description: Data backup, replication, and integrity recovery

hasDefinedTerm.name: AI-Model-Failover
hasDefinedTerm.description: AI model failover to backup model or human-in-the-loop

hasDefinedTerm.name: AI-Pipeline-Recovery
hasDefinedTerm.description: AI/ML pipeline recovery — training data, model retraining, feature store

hasDefinedTerm.name: Low
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risk score 1-4 — manage by routine procedures

hasDefinedTerm.name: Medium
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risk score 5-9 — manage by specific monitoring

hasDefinedTerm.name: High
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risk score 10-15 — senior management attention

hasDefinedTerm.name: Critical
hasDefinedTerm.description: Risk score 16-25 — board-level attention, immediate action

oaa:enumerations.name: RiskVelocity
oaa:enumerations.description: Speed at which a risk could materialise

hasDefinedTerm.name: Immediate
hasDefinedTerm.description: Impact within hours

hasDefinedTerm.name: Days
hasDefinedTerm.description: Impact within 1-7 days

hasDefinedTerm.name: Weeks
hasDefinedTerm.description: Impact within 1-4 weeks

hasDefinedTerm.name: Months
hasDefinedTerm.description: Impact over 1-6 months

hasDefinedTerm.name: Years
hasDefinedTerm.description: Impact develops over years

oaa:enumerations.name: AssessmentType
oaa:enumerations.description: Types of enterprise risk assessment exercises

hasDefinedTerm.name: Enterprise-Wide
hasDefinedTerm.description: Full enterprise risk assessment

hasDefinedTerm.name: Domain-Specific
hasDefinedTerm.description: Assessment focused on a specific risk domain

hasDefinedTerm.name: Project
hasDefinedTerm.description: Project-level risk assessment

hasDefinedTerm.name: Vendor
hasDefinedTerm.description: Vendor or third-party risk assessment

hasDefinedTerm.name: AI-System
hasDefinedTerm.description: AI system risk assessment — EU AI Act conformity

hasDefinedTerm.name: Regulatory-Change
hasDefinedTerm.description: Assessment triggered by regulatory change

hasDefinedTerm.name: Incident-Triggered
hasDefinedTerm.description: Post-incident risk reassessment

bridges.description: RiskRegister governed by GRC governance context
Risk assessed by governance assurance activities
Enterprise risk specialises into ISO 27005 infosec risk
Enterprise assessment specialises into ISO 27005 assessment
Enterprise control specialises into ISO 27001 Annex A control
Risk register assessed by CAF Objective A outcomes
Enterprise control aligns to MCSB security benchmark
VSEM bridge — risk threatens strategic objective

crossOntologyReferences.description: ERM-ONT bridges UP to GRC-FW (governance oversight), DOWN to RMF-IS27005 (Tier 2 ISO 27005 specialisation), ACROSS to NCSC-CAF (cyber risk assessment) and MCSB (security benchmark), and VSEM to VSOM (risk-threatens-strategy)

changeControl.changeNotes: Initial release: 6 owned entities (RiskRegister, Risk, RiskAppetite, Control, ResilienceCapability, RiskAssessment). 19 relationships (11 internal + 8 cross-ontology including 1 VSEM bridge). 15 business rules. 11 enumerations. 8 join patterns. ISO 31000:2018 aligned. 23 risk categories including 9 AI-specific. Satisfies grc-fw:governsRisk and grc-fw:assuranceAssessesRisk forward references. Epic 30 F30.2.

versionHistory.notes: Initial release. Enterprise risk management aligned to ISO 31000:2018, COBIT APO12/EDM03, NCSC Cyber Gov Code Principle A, FCA/PRA Operational Resilience, DORA Chapter II, NIS2 Article 21. Tier 1 parent for RMF-IS27005 (Tier 2). Single authoritative source for enterprise controls across GRC. Epic 30 F30.2.
